items.title: Twee vliegen in één klap voor Di Lorenzo: Napoli-aanvoerder onder het mes
items.summary: Nu Napoli aanvoerder Giovanni Di Lorenzo toch een poos moet missen door een zware knieblessure, maken de Partenopei meteen van de gelegenheid gebruik om de verdediger verder op te lappen. Di Lorenzo zal ook geopereerd worden aan een slepende blessure in zijn voet.

items.title: Go Ahead pakt na Deadline Day uit met drie contractverlengingen
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles zit niet stil nu de winterse transferperiode erop zit. De club uit Deventer maakte woensdag bekend dat de contracten van Yassir Salah Rahmouni, Julius Dirksen en Thibo Baeten zijn verlengd tot de zomer van 2029.

items.title: Harper is the fight Dubois has been waiting for: 'So much on the line'
items.summary: Caroline Dubois has finally got the fight she has been waiting for after her lightweight world title unification bout with Terri Harper was confirmed.

items.title: Draper: I was in a hole mentally with 'complex' injury - but I'm ready to compete now
items.summary: Britain's Jack Draper has revealed to Sky Sports he was "in a hole" due to the "complex" nature of his arm injury, but stresses he is now ready to compete and is set on defending his Indian Wells title.

items.title: T20 World Cup Group B: Ireland among injury-hit Australia's rivals
items.summary: We look at the sides in Group B - Australia, Ireland, Sri Lanka, Oman and Zimbabwe, - for the T20 World Cup, which is live on Sky Sports from February 7 to March 8.

items.title: Ajax Onder-19 mist topscorer in Polen en is klaar in de Youth League
items.summary: Ajax Onder-19 moet afscheid nemen van de Youth League. In Polen gingen de Amsterdamse talenten met 2-1 onderuit tegen de leeftijdsgenoten van Legia Warschau.

items.title: Arteta: 'Magical' final is 'best vitamins' for Arsenal
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has described Arsenal 's "magical" achievement in reaching the Carabao Cup Final as "the best vitamins we can put in our bodies."

items.title: Araujo hugs Flick after goal in 'step by step' return
items.summary: Hansi Flick said Barcelona will continue to support Ronald Araújo after the defender netted on his first start for the club since returning from a mental health break against Albacete in the Copa del Rey on Tuesday.

items.title: Newell's have dream plan to lure Messi in 2027
items.summary: Newell's Old Boys are working on a project to entice Lionel Messi to play for the club in the first half of 2027.

items.title: Arsenal's Zubimendi rejects claim Madrid need him
items.summary: Arsenal midfielder Martín Zubimendi has denied speculation in Spain that he is the player that Real Madrid needs.

items.title: Inter ontvangt flinke boete na vuurwerkincident met doelman Audero
items.summary: De Italiaanse sportrechter heeft Internazionale een boete van vijftigduizend euro opgelegd naar aanleiding van het vuurwerkincident, waarbij een fan van de Nerazzuri een rotje richting Cremonese-doelman Emil Audero gooide. De Italiaanse sluitpost greep meteen naar zijn oor, waarna het spel tijdelijk werd stilgelegd.

items.title: België wil met opmerkelijke pilot fans gecontroleerd vuurwerk af laten steken
items.summary: De Belgische overheid gaat de wetgeving omtrent illegaal vuurwerk in voetbalstadions strenger maken, maar start tegelijkertijd met een pilot om te onderzoeken of vuurwerk gecontroleerd afgestoken zou kunnen worden. Dit alleen door fans, die daarvoor speciaal zijn opgeleid. Het Belgische nazorg- en onderzoekscentrum voor mensen met brandwonden kan daar met de pet niet bij.

items.title: Kartal books quarter-final spot with win over Zarazua in Abu Dhabi
items.summary: Highlights of Renata Zarazua against Sonay Kartal at the Abu Dhabi Open.

items.title: The goalscoring Spurs defender making headlines in Germany
items.summary: Luka Vuskovic has earned a reputation for scoring goals on loan at Hamburg, but just how good is the teenage Tottenham defender?

items.title: 'He's better than that' - Wilson joining Spurs a bad decision, says McInnes
items.summary: Hearts manager Derek McInnes believes teenage striker James Wilson has made a mistake by joining Tottenham on loan.

items.title: Highlights: Boulter dismantles Golubic in straight-sets victory
items.summary: Highlights of Katie Boulter against Viktorija Golubic at the Ostrava Open.

items.title: Is Guardiola right about Man City's lower net spend?
items.summary: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said his team only sit seventh on net spend over the last five years. Is he right?

items.title: The F1, football and NFL figures who kept Epstein links after conviction
items.summary: Senior figures in sport maintained personal contact with Jeffrey Epstein, while others were discussed regarding potential deals.

items.title: Transfer window verdict: how every Women’s Super League club fared
items.summary: After impressive work by Manchester United and Liverpool and disappointment for Chelsea, we assess every team’s business With so many senior players’ contracts expiring in June, Arsenal’s focus was on preparing for the summer, when they are expected to go through a major rebuild. Therefore their quiet window was no surprise, but they will be relatively pleased to have brought in a star of the future, Smilla Holmberg, at right-back and to have fulfilled their need for a backup goalkeeper, with Barbora Votíkova’s deadline-day loan. Much more significant, though, is the positive progress they are understood to have made in their attempt to sign Georgia Stanway on a free at the end of the season, and big decisions such as not seeking to extend Katie McCabe’s stay, as they prepare to refresh the team. Continue reading...

items.title: What wobble? Arsenal break hoodoo to reach first final in six years
items.summary: Arsenal will play in their first cup final in six years, aiming to put their recent lack of silverware behind them by lifting the Carabao Cup at Wembley.
